title: uragoga calva
intro: Uragoga calva, commonly known as the "monkey's pepper", is a plant species in the family Annonaceae. It is traditionally used in West African folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Uragoga calva include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing pain, fever, and inflammation. The therapeutic actions of this plant include its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds.
side_effects: Uragoga calva can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach cramps due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged use or excessive consumption can lead to more severe symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and even respiratory problems. Some individuals may also experience allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing. It is essential to use Uragoga calva under the guidance of a medical professional to minimize the risk of these side effects.
